rag_rules: Place a generated annex block in the declaration scope where every referenced name is visible.
Keep type-owned contract content with the component type, and keep implementation-only bindings with the implementation after the elements they depend on.
generic_rules: Fuse new annex content into the existing owner without changing unrelated declarations or architecture.
Preserve original subcomponents, connections, properties, ordering, and surrounding structure as much as possible.
Do not shift content between type and implementation scopes unless visibility or dependency requires it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Preserve the annex wrapper and declaration terminators exactly when inserting a generated block.
anti_patterns: Do not place subcomponent-path references or other implementation-only names into a type-level annex.
Do not duplicate existing annex blocks or duplicate existing contract content during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: agree_syntax_notes on temporal recurrence, branching, and logical operators
type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
